skill.name: reasoningbank-with-agentdb
skill.description: Build adaptive agent memory with ReasoningBank patterns and AgentDB storage for trajectories, verdicts, distilled memories, and pattern retrieval.
skill.summary: Use AgentDB-backed ReasoningBank patterns to help agents learn from past execution outcomes.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, filesystem, network, and weak-crypto patterns. Review shows this is a documentation-only skill with markdown command examples, local database paths, and external project links, not confirmed malicious code. Publication is acceptable with warnings because the instructions ask users to run remote npm packages and handle local agent memory databases.

medium_findings.title: Remote Package Execution in Setup Instructions
medium_findings.description: The skill documents commands that run agentdb through npx with the latest tag and add an MCP server. This is a legitimate setup pattern, but users would execute code fetched from npm and should verify the package source and version.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and would run external npm tooling if copied. Confidence is not higher because the commands are documentation, not hidden executable code.

medium_findings.title: Local Agent Memory Databases May Contain Sensitive Data
medium_findings.description: The instructions create, migrate, export, and inspect local ReasoningBank databases. These databases can contain prompts, trajectories, outcomes, metrics, and distilled memories, so users should protect backups and migration sources.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The database paths and export or migration flows are clearly documented. The sensitivity depends on the user's stored agent experiences, so the issue is a handling risk rather than direct exfiltration.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to the AgentDB GitHub project and website. These links are expected documentation references, with no evidence of automatic network access or data transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the Learn More section. No code in the skill sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography blockers, but the cited lines describe ReasoningBank, decision-making, and reasoning modules. No cryptographic algorithm use was found at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text contains descriptive prose, not crypto code or algorithm selection. This strongly indicates substring-based false positives.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Command Blocks Execute External Tools When Copied
dangerous_patterns.description: Several markdown examples use npx and claude CLI commands. They are not executed by the skill itself, but they can change local configuration and run external packages when users copy them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible setup examples and are relevant to the skill. They are risky only after user execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Local State Directories
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples use hidden directories such as .agentdb and .swarm for persistent memory state. Hidden local state can be overlooked during backups, sharing, or cleanup.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden paths are explicitly present in setup and troubleshooting commands. The concern is data governance, not covert file access.

content.user_title: Build Adaptive Agent Memory with AgentDB
content.value_statement: Agents often repeat mistakes because past outcomes are not organized for reuse. This skill shows how to store trajectories, judge results, and retrieve useful memories with AgentDB.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to initialize an AgentDB-backed ReasoningBank database.
Shows patterns for storing successful experiences and execution trajectories.
Demonstrates verdict judgment using similar past outcomes.
Documents memory distillation and consolidation workflows.
Covers migration from legacy ReasoningBank memory files.
Shows MCP setup commands for Claude Code integration.
limitations: Requires Node.js and the external AgentDB package.
Does not provide runnable source files beyond examples in SKILL.md.
Requires users to manage privacy for stored trajectories and exports.
Performance claims depend on the installed AgentDB version and workload.

use_cases.title: Improve Agent Decision Quality
use_cases.description: Store successful and failed task trajectories so future agents can compare new tasks with past outcomes.

use_cases.title: Migrate Legacy ReasoningBank Data
use_cases.description: Move older ReasoningBank memory into AgentDB and validate the resulting database statistics.

use_cases.title: Prototype Experience Replay
use_cases.description: Build a local memory loop that retrieves similar experiences before judging or refining an agent action.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the safest steps for adding AgentDB-backed ReasoningBank memory to my new agent project.

prompt_templates.title: Design Trajectory Tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a trajectory tracking plan for my agent, including stored fields, outcomes, and retrieval criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a migration checklist from legacy ReasoningBank memory to AgentDB, including validation and backup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Architect Adaptive Memory
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to propose an adaptive memory architecture with trajectory judgment, memory distillation, and cross-domain pattern reuse.

output_examples.input: Design ReasoningBank memory for an API optimization agent.
output: A trajectory schema with task, actions, outcome, and latency metrics.
A retrieval plan that filters by optimization domain and confidence.
A distillation step that summarizes repeated successful fixes.

output_examples.input: Assess whether I should migrate my legacy memory file.
output: A migration checklist covering backup, conversion, statistics review, and rollback.
A warning to inspect stored prompts and trajectories before exporting data.
A recommendation to pin package versions before running migration commands.

best_practices: Pin AgentDB versions instead of running latest in production workflows.
Review memory database contents before sharing backups or exports.
Store outcome metrics consistently so verdict judgment remains meaningful.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, credentials, or private customer data in trajectories.
Do not trust retrieved memories without checking confidence and recency.
Do not run migration or MCP commands without verifying the package source.

faq.question: Does this skill install AgentDB automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands and API examples that users may run after reviewing the package source.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes MCP setup commands intended for Claude Code integration.

faq.question: What data does ReasoningBank store?
faq.answer: It can store task trajectories, outcomes, metrics, embeddings, distilled patterns, and confidence values.

faq.question: Is this only for reinforcement learning experts?
faq.answer: No. The examples explain core concepts, but prior familiarity with agent memory helps.

faq.question: Can I migrate existing memory data?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents migration commands from a legacy ReasoningBank memory database.

faq.question: What security precautions should I take?
faq.answer: Pin package versions, inspect database contents, protect backups, and avoid storing secrets in memories.
